reasoning: The failure is attributed to severe technical degradation of the A1 interface, characterized by high latency and packet retransmissions. This caused a policy delivery failure from Non-RT RIC to Near-RT RIC, leading to suboptimal RAN configuration and subsequent performance degradation in O-CU and O-DU via E2 and F1 interfaces.
actionable_steps: 1. Verify Transport Path: Inspect the physical and virtual network path between the Non-RT RIC and the Near-RT RIC for congestion, packet loss, or faulty hardware. 2. Analyze Near-RT RIC Resources: Check CPU and memory utilization on the Near-RT RIC to ensure it is not overloading and dropping A1 control plane packets. 3. Reset A1 Session: Attempt to restart the A1 signaling session to clear any stale state or transient communication errors.
